7M-most-startups-are-undercharging.author: Dalton Caldwell
7M-most-startups-are-undercharging.description: Dalton Caldwell, Y Combinator partner and founder of imeem and Mixed Media Labs, on pricing and how most startups undercharge. He also shares examples of how companies like Doordash, Zapier and Dropbox, determined their pricing strategy.

7L-how-do-you-create-luck.title: How do you create luck?
7L-how-do-you-create-luck.author: Dalton Caldwell
7L-how-do-you-create-luck.description: To create luck, you need to move fast, make things, and give yourself as many opportunities as possible. YC Partner Dalton Caldwell shares his advice for founders, with examples from companies like Brex and Retool.

7H-design-for-startups-part-2.title: Design for startups (part 2)
7H-design-for-startups-part-2.author: Garry Tan
7H-design-for-startups-part-2.description: YC's Garry Tan describes how to effectively integrate design into a product development process.

7G-design-for-startups-part-1.title: Design for startups (part 1)
7G-design-for-startups-part-1.author: Garry Tan
7G-design-for-startups-part-1.description: YC Partner Garry Tan, co-founder of Initialized Capital and a design expert describes how to effectively integrate design and design thinking into a product development process.  This was a talk for YC's Startup School in 2018.

77-how-to-invent-the-future-part-1.title: How to invent the future (part 1)
77-how-to-invent-the-future-part-1.author: Alan Kay
77-how-to-invent-the-future-part-1.description: One of the pioneers of personal computing, Alan Kay shares his story and how to think about building for the future. From YC's Startup School in 2017.

76-how-to-invent-the-future-part-2.title: How to invent the future (part 2)
76-how-to-invent-the-future-part-2.author: Alan Kay
76-how-to-invent-the-future-part-2.description: Alan Kay, one of the pioneers of personal computing, speaks about the history of the technology we know today.

73-how-to-start-a-startup-building-for-t.title: How to Start a Startup: Building for the Enterprise
73-how-to-start-a-startup-building-for-t.author: Aaron Levie
73-how-to-start-a-startup-building-for-t.description: Aaron Levie, CEO and Co-founder of Box talks about his lessons learned building an enterprise software company, and why he made the decision to change their business model to what it is today. From Startup School 2017.

70-template-sales-agreement.title: Template sales agreement
70-template-sales-agreement.author: Carolynn Levy
70-template-sales-agreement.description: Y Combinator's open-source sales template agreement.

6r-building-culture.title: Building culture
6r-building-culture.author: Tim Brady
6r-building-culture.description: YC Group Partner Tim Brady covers the importance of building a strong and coherent culture early and shares six things that you can do now to help you create a solid foundation for your startup.

6m-understanding-safes-and-priced-equity.title: Understanding SAFEs and priced equity rounds
6m-understanding-safes-and-priced-equity.author: Kirsty Nathoo
6m-understanding-safes-and-priced-equity.description: YC's Kirsty Nathoo gives the lowdown on several different ways to capitalize your company and how those impact founder equity and cap tables overall.

6l-how-to-improve-conversion-rates.title: How to improve conversion rates
6l-how-to-improve-conversion-rates.author: Kevin Hale
6l-how-to-improve-conversion-rates.description: YC's Kevin Hale covers the first principles of conversion, one of the main drivers of growth for your startup, and how you can improve it starting with your landing page.

6h-startup-pricing-101.title: Startup pricing 101
6h-startup-pricing-101.author: Kevin Hale
6h-startup-pricing-101.description: YC's Kevin Hale goes over the fundamentals of pricing and monetization, how it affects your customer acquisition strategy, and how to optimize it through a few rules of thumb.

6a-on-starting-and-scaling-direct-mail-a.title: On starting and scaling direct mail automation startup Lob
6a-on-starting-and-scaling-direct-mail-a.author: Harry Zhang
6a-on-starting-and-scaling-direct-mail-a.description: Harry Zhang, CEO & founder of Lob (YC S13), talks about his journey as a founder, the genesis and evolution of Lob, and lessons he's learned along the way.

6S-on-starting-and-scaling-dropbox-yc-w0.title: On starting and scaling Dropbox (YC W07)
6S-on-starting-and-scaling-dropbox-yc-w0.author: Drew Houston
6S-on-starting-and-scaling-dropbox-yc-w0.description: Drew Houston, co-founder & CEO of Dropbox (YC W07) talks about how he started Dropbox and went through YC after being rejected once and how has since grown it to over 500 million users today.

6R-how-pachama-uses-tech-to-tackle-clima.title: How Pachama uses tech to tackle climate change
6R-how-pachama-uses-tech-to-tackle-clima.author: Diego Saez Gil
6R-how-pachama-uses-tech-to-tackle-clima.description: Diego Saez Gil is the founder of Pachama (YC W19). Pachama is building a marketplace where companies can support carbon offset projects. He discusses the genesis of the idea and how climate change is a big business problem to work on.

6Q-diane-greene-s-advice-for-founders.title: Diane Greene's advice for founders
6Q-diane-greene-s-advice-for-founders.author: Diane Greene
6Q-diane-greene-s-advice-for-founders.description: Diane Greene has been the co-founder and CEO of three startups, including VMWare. She talks about lessons learned that has helped her along her founder journey.

6N-vinod-khosla-on-how-to-build-the-futu.title: Vinod Khosla on How to Build the Future
6N-vinod-khosla-on-how-to-build-the-futu.author: Vinod Khosla
6N-vinod-khosla-on-how-to-build-the-futu.description: Vinod Khosla, founding CEO of Sun Microsystems and founder of Khosla Ventures, talks on the importance of hiring and picking the right people, being generous with early employee equity, and his gripes with other investors.

6M-on-starting-and-scaling-indian-shoppi.title: On starting and scaling Indian shopping site Meesho
6M-on-starting-and-scaling-indian-shoppi.author: Vidit Aatrey
6M-on-starting-and-scaling-indian-shoppi.description: Vidit Aatrey is the co-founder and CEO of Meesho (YC S16), the most downloaded shopping app in India. He talks about how Meesho started and pivoted, how he thinks about building a business in India and how his role has changed.

6L-on-starting-and-scaling-construction-.title: On starting and scaling construction startup PlanGrid
6L-on-starting-and-scaling-construction-.author: Tracy Young
6L-on-starting-and-scaling-construction-.description: Tracy Young, CEO & co-founder of PlanGrid (YC W12), talks about her journey as a founder and lessons she's learned from scaling PlanGrid to 400+ people.

6I-on-starting-and-scaling-recruiting-so.title: On starting and scaling recruiting software startup Lever
6I-on-starting-and-scaling-recruiting-so.author: Sarah Nahm
6I-on-starting-and-scaling-recruiting-so.description: Sarah Nahm, CEO & co-founder of Lever (YC S12), discusses her journey to becoming a founder, the decision to leave Google to build Lever, initial challenges of the startup, and lessons learned along the way about hiring and diversity.

69-on-starting-and-scaling-meal-kit-comp.title: On starting and scaling meal kit company Gobble
69-on-starting-and-scaling-meal-kit-comp.author: Ooshma Garg
69-on-starting-and-scaling-meal-kit-comp.description: Ooshma Garg, the founder and CEO of dinner kit delivery service Gobble (YC W14), details her company's complicated years-long path to what might look from the outside like simple success, and described how building a startup is like a daily game of Snakes and Ladders that can take you two steps forward, and then two steps back. She also gave her best advice for other entrepreneurs.

68-product-and-design-process-for-remote.title: Product and design process for remote teams
68-product-and-design-process-for-remote.author: Mike Knoop
68-product-and-design-process-for-remote.description: Mike Knoop, co-founder and head of product of Zapier (YC S12), talks about how to jumpstart a SaaS ecosystem, the decision to go fully remote and how to manage a remote team.

67-user-you-don-t-want.title: User you don't want
67-user-you-don-t-want.author: Michael Seibel
67-user-you-don-t-want.description: Many startups will take every user they can get in the beginning. But this may cause all sorts of problems. Some of them will try to hijack your product to solve a problem you didn't intend to solve. By and large, these hijackers are users you don't want. Learn how to identify and avoid this.

65-how-to-cold-email-investors.title: How to cold email investors
65-how-to-cold-email-investors.author: Michael Seibel
65-how-to-cold-email-investors.description: The best way to communicate with investors over cold email is simple: make it short. Your goal is to get a reply. Here are a few components to also include and consider to optimize for this.

64-what-makes-great-founders-stand-out.title: What makes great founders stand out?
64-what-makes-great-founders-stand-out.author: Michael Seibel
64-what-makes-great-founders-stand-out.description: It's a short list: Good communication. Great execution. And motivation. Being relentlessly resource creates formidability. Here's why.

63-why-fundraising-is-different-in-silic.title: Why fundraising is different in Silicon Valley
63-why-fundraising-is-different-in-silic.author: Michael Seibel
63-why-fundraising-is-different-in-silic.description: Investors are not spread evenly around the world; they are concentrated in Sillicon Valley. Each has much more likely passed on a big deal and it will make them pause each time moving forward. This means there's a different, more positive mentality to funding startups.

62-how-to-build-product-as-a-small-start.title: How to build product as a small startup
62-how-to-build-product-as-a-small-start.author: Michael Seibel
62-how-to-build-product-as-a-small-start.description: Founders tend to debate more than build and iterate and get stuck. Here's a process to try to get product out of the door quickly.

61-order-of-operations-for-starting-a-st.title: Order of operations for starting a startup
61-order-of-operations-for-starting-a-st.author: Michael Seibel
61-order-of-operations-for-starting-a-st.description: When and how should you start a startup? This guide will help you walk the path from nothing to a launched minimal viable product (MVP).

60-should-i-start-a-startup.title: Should I start a startup?
60-should-i-start-a-startup.author: Michael Seibel
60-should-i-start-a-startup.description: Are you cut out to be a startup founder? Here are some questions to ask yourself.

5z-the-real-product-market-fit.title: The real product-market fit
5z-the-real-product-market-fit.author: Michael Seibel
5z-the-real-product-market-fit.description: Founders often believe they've found product/market fit when they haven't. This is a huge problem because they start hiring people, increasing burn, and optimizing their product before they've actually discovered what needs to be built. Here's a way to understand when you've really found product/market fit.

5p-creating-good-company-culture-and-sti.title: Creating good company culture (and sticking to it)
5p-creating-good-company-culture-and-sti.author: Kathryn Minshew
5p-creating-good-company-culture-and-sti.description: Kathryn Minshew, the co-founder of the job search and career advice platform The Muse (YC W12), shares four key pieces of advice she's learned as a founder so far, including being painfully honest about the state of your startup, how crucial it is to construct a positive company culture, and staying motivated by remembering your roots.

5n-why-you-should-or-should-not-work-at-.title: Why you should or should not work at a startup
5n-why-you-should-or-should-not-work-at-.author: Justin Kan
5n-why-you-should-or-should-not-work-at-.description: Justin Kan, co-founder of Twitch and former YC partner, discusses why you should, but also why you shouldn't join a startup.

5l-how-not-to-fail.title: How not to fail
5l-how-not-to-fail.author: Jessica Livingston
5l-how-not-to-fail.description: Jessica Livingston has seen over 1000 companies go through YC and shares her learnings about what it takes to succeed as a founder. She emphasizes the importance of avoiding distraction and making something people want.

5j-jeff-dean-on-building-intelligent-sys.title: Jeff Dean on building intelligent systems with large scale deep learning
5j-jeff-dean-on-building-intelligent-sys.author: Jeff Dean
5j-jeff-dean-on-building-intelligent-sys.description: Jeff Dean is a Google Senior Fellow in the Research Group, where he leads the Google Brain project. He discusses state of the art machine learning research, state of Tensorflow, real-world applications he's excited about, and more.

5f-on-starting-and-scaling-one-of-the-bi.title: On starting and scaling one of the biggest iOS apps
5f-on-starting-and-scaling-one-of-the-bi.author: David Lieb
5f-on-starting-and-scaling-one-of-the-bi.description: David Lieb, founder of Bump (YC S09) and former head of Google Photos, talks about his journey as a founder and how Bump became one of the biggest apps on the App Store. He discusses how Bump pivoted from contact sharing to photo app, and his transition to scaling today's biggest photo app, Google Photos.

5b-how-do-you-convince-someone-to-join-y.title: How do you convince someone to join your startup?
5b-how-do-you-convince-someone-to-join-y.author: Dalton Caldwell
5b-how-do-you-convince-someone-to-join-y.description: You must first convince yourself of your own idea. People can tell if you don't believe in what you're doing. Your confidence will flow out to others, including potential co-founders, employees and customers. How do you convince yourself?

5a-consume-information-that-encourages-y.title: Consume information that encourages you to do more
5a-consume-information-that-encourages-y.author: Dalton Caldwell
5a-consume-information-that-encourages-y.description: There is a lot of information out there geared towards aspiring founders. A lot of it is not good and discouraging. Here's a way to consume the right information to help you build your future and startup.

5Q-on-starting-and-scaling-aerospace-com.title: On starting and scaling aerospace company Boom
5Q-on-starting-and-scaling-aerospace-com.author: Blake Scholl
5Q-on-starting-and-scaling-aerospace-com.description: Blake Scholl is founder and CEO of Boom Technology (YC W16). He discusses deciding to start a hard tech company, innovation in aviation, applying to YC, advice for hard tech companies and more.

5O-building-a-startup-is-about-solving-a.title: Building a startup is about solving a problem
5O-building-a-startup-is-about-solving-a.author: Avni Patel Thompson
5O-building-a-startup-is-about-solving-a.description: This is a story of how one founder, Avni Patel, struggled with her first startup but eventually discovered a problem many people had that was unsolved.

5N-on-starting-and-scaling-bloom-institu.title: On starting and scaling Bloom Institute of Technology
5N-on-starting-and-scaling-bloom-institu.author: Austen Allred
5N-on-starting-and-scaling-bloom-institu.description: The Bloom Institute of Technology (YC S17) provides a CS education that's free until you get a job. Austen Allred, CEO & founder, discusses his journey as a founder, including how the idea formed, what he thinks about copycats and the decision to be remote.

5I-on-starting-and-scaling-healthcare-st.title: On starting and scaling healthcare startup 23andMe
5I-on-starting-and-scaling-healthcare-st.author: Anne Wojcicki
5I-on-starting-and-scaling-healthcare-st.description: Anne Wojcicki, co-founder & CEO of 23andMe, on why and how she started and grew 23andMe. Anne talks about the best decisions she's made and the struggles of running a company in a regulated industry. She also covers the importance of thoughtful hiring, communication, and persistance.

5G-on-raising-seed-and-series-a-as-a-wom.title: On raising Seed and Series A as a woman founder
5G-on-raising-seed-and-series-a-as-a-wom.author: Amber Atherton, Iba Masood
5G-on-raising-seed-and-series-a-as-a-wom.description: Two women founders talk about their fundraising histories and what they've learned about the process.

4x-on-starting-a-company-from-a-hackatho.title: On starting a company from a hackathon project
4x-on-starting-a-company-from-a-hackatho.author: Tanay Tandon
4x-on-starting-a-company-from-a-hackatho.description: Athelas (YC S16) makes low cost devices that enable rapid blood diagnostics. Here's the story of how Athelas began as a small hardware prototype at a YC Hackathon and grew into a real shippable product.

4s-navigating-success-for-your-startup.title: Navigating success for your startup
4s-navigating-success-for-your-startup.author: Sam Altman
4s-navigating-success-for-your-startup.description: Different people accept different outcomes for their startup; it's all a matter of what you want to do. If you want to shoot for the billion dollar outcome, then perhaps fundraising from VCs is the right move. But if you don't or what you have cannot get there, then sometimes it's much better for you to not sell any of your equity.

4q-later-stage-advice-for-startups.title: Later stage advice for startups
4q-later-stage-advice-for-startups.author: Sam Altman
4q-later-stage-advice-for-startups.description: There are many common issues founders face in the later stages of their startups. Here's a condensed list of what they are and how to get ahead of them sooner than later.

4p-before-growing-your-startup.title: Before growing your startup
4p-before-growing-your-startup.author: Sam Altman
4p-before-growing-your-startup.description: Startups are defined by growth, but growth isn't step one in building a great company. If you focus on trying to grow before you make a product people love, you are unlikely to succeed.

4m-enterprise-sales-for-hackers.title: Enterprise sales for hackers
4m-enterprise-sales-for-hackers.author: Ryan Junee
4m-enterprise-sales-for-hackers.description: Many hackers-turned-founders understand that sales are an important and valuable skill, but shy away from doing it out of fear and uncertainty. But being a great enterprise salesperson requires the same skill set as being a great hacker — you just need to reframe how you think about sales.

4l-advice-for-job-seekers-on-resumes-and.title: Advice for job seekers on resumes and reaching out
4l-advice-for-job-seekers-on-resumes-and.author: Ryan Choi
4l-advice-for-job-seekers-on-resumes-and.description: To help job seekers, YC's Ryan Choi spends a lot of time doing 1-1s and resume reviews. Here are his most common pieces of advice for those on the job search.

4e-guide-to-product-development.title: Guide to product development
4e-guide-to-product-development.author: Michael Seibel
4e-guide-to-product-development.description: This is a guide to running your product development process. This will help you rapidly iterate, measure, test, and improve your product while fully engaging your team.

4d-a-letter-to-college-students.title: A letter to college students
4d-a-letter-to-college-students.author: Michael Seibel
4d-a-letter-to-college-students.description: These are the decisions people commonly make during college that have the most negative effects on their ability to start a startup. Avoid them.

4c-getting-press-for-your-startup.title: Getting press for your startup
4c-getting-press-for-your-startup.author: Michael Seibel
4c-getting-press-for-your-startup.description: Treating PR like business development means developing relationships with reporters as people, getting introduced through warm connections instead of cold emails, and creating a fair value exchange. Here's the process YC companies follow when they're ready for press.

4P-a-standard-and-clean-series-a-term-sh.title: A standard and clean Series A term sheet
4P-a-standard-and-clean-series-a-term-sh.author: Jason Kwon, Aaron Harris
4P-a-standard-and-clean-series-a-term-sh.description: YC has seen thousands of Series A term sheets, and we know what good terms look like. This is what a Series A term sheet looks like with standard and clean terms from a good Silicon Valley VC.

4L-how-biotech-startup-funding-will-chan.title: How biotech startup funding will change in the next 10 years
4L-how-biotech-startup-funding-will-chan.author: Jared Friedman
4L-how-biotech-startup-funding-will-chan.description: The landscape of funding for biotech startups is changing. What happened for software companies 10 years ago is happening to biotech today. Here's why more people will be able to start biotech companies moving forward.

4H-how-to-hire-your-first-engineer.title: How to hire your first engineer
4H-how-to-hire-your-first-engineer.author: Harj Taggar
4H-how-to-hire-your-first-engineer.description: Here is advice for early stage startup founders who are hiring their first engineer.

4G-convincing-engineers-to-join-your-tea.title: Convincing engineers to join your team
4G-convincing-engineers-to-join-your-tea.author: Harj Taggar
4G-convincing-engineers-to-join-your-tea.description: Once you've found an engineer you want to hire, the final step is presenting them an offer to join your team and convincing them to accept it. This is advice for increasing the percentage of the offers you make that are accepted.

3x-the-hidden-forces-behind-china-s-cont.title: The hidden forces behind China's content king Toutiao
3x-the-hidden-forces-behind-china-s-cont.author: Anu Hariharan
3x-the-hidden-forces-behind-china-s-cont.description: Toutiao, a news aggregator, has quickly become one of the top apps in China. Learn how it grew into its popularity and why users use it more often than any other daily app.

3w-how-to-create-and-manage-a-board.title: How to create and manage a board
3w-how-to-create-and-manage-a-board.author: Anu Hariharan
3w-how-to-create-and-manage-a-board.description: Best practices in creating and managing a board, including how to create board decks, preparing and running board meetings, and overcoming board issues.

3t-how-wechat-grew-to-be-the-1-app-in-th.title: How WeChat grew to be the #1 app in the world
3t-how-wechat-grew-to-be-the-1-app-in-th.author: Anu Hariharan
3t-how-wechat-grew-to-be-the-1-app-in-th.description: Here is a deep dive into the growth strategy that led to WeChat scaling to over a billion users.

3k-the-second-job-of-a-startup-ceo.title: The second job of a startup CEO
3k-the-second-job-of-a-startup-ceo.author: Ali Rowghani
3k-the-second-job-of-a-startup-ceo.description: A CEO's first job is to build a product users love; the second job is to build a company to maximize the opportunity that the product has surfaced. The future of your startup depends on how you transition from phase one to phase two.

3j-how-to-measure-leadership.title: How to measure leadership
3j-how-to-measure-leadership.author: Ali Rowghani
3j-how-to-measure-leadership.description: Measuring leadership through bottom-line company performance also fails to provide any clues as to how someone can improve as a leader. So is there a better way?

3h-series-a-diligence-checklist.title: Series A diligence checklist
3h-series-a-diligence-checklist.author: Aaron Harris, Jason Kwon
3h-series-a-diligence-checklist.description: This checklist includes all the information you'll need to have ready once you sign a term sheet. Having all of this together in one place before you sign a term sheet will cut as much as a week off of your closing process.

3Z-advice-for-companies-with-less-than-1.title: Advice for companies with less than 1 year of runway
3Z-advice-for-companies-with-less-than-1.author: Dalton Caldwell
3Z-advice-for-companies-with-less-than-1.description: Having low runaway is a tricky spot to be in. Many founders delude themselves into thinking they can raise more money to get out of it. Here we discuss why this is bad and other counter-productive ideas founders have.

2z-pinduoduo-and-the-rise-of-social-e-co.title: Pinduoduo and the rise of social e-commerce
2z-pinduoduo-and-the-rise-of-social-e-co.author: Anu Hariharan, Nic Dardenne
2z-pinduoduo-and-the-rise-of-social-e-co.description: The success of Pinduoduo in China suggests there is a huge opportunity for social commerce platforms to emerge in other regions. Learn how Pinduoduo grew so quickly by creating a new category in social shopping.

2s-advice-for-new-managers.title: Advice for new managers
2s-advice-for-new-managers.author: Aaron Epstein
2s-advice-for-new-managers.description: One of the most difficult things about starting a company is that you have to create both a product that people love and a company where people want to work at the same time. Here's advice for those just beginning to grow and manage a team.

NP-fran-ois-chollet-arc-agi-3-beyond-dee.title: François Chollet: ARC-AGI-3, Beyond Deep Learning & A New Approach To ML
NP-fran-ois-chollet-arc-agi-3-beyond-dee.author: Y Combinator
NP-fran-ois-chollet-arc-agi-3-beyond-dee.description: François Chollet has spent years asking a different question than most of the AI world. Instead of scaling what already works, he’s trying to understand what intelligence actually is—and how to build it from first principles.

NO-inside-the-startup-reinventing-americ.title: Inside The Startup Reinventing America’s Trillion Dollar Chemical Industry
NO-inside-the-startup-reinventing-americ.author: Jared Friedman
NO-inside-the-startup-reinventing-americ.description: In this episode of Hard Tech, YC's Jared Friedman visits co-founders Gaurab Chakrabarti and Sean Hunt at their Houston HQ to see how they went from a $7,000 PVC reactor to a billion-dollar company competing with industry giants.

NN-agents-for-non-technical-users.title: Agents For Non-Technical Users
NN-agents-for-non-technical-users.author: Y Combinator
NN-agents-for-non-technical-users.description: Emergent lets anyone build and ship production-ready software. It's one of the fastest-growing platforms to do it: in just eight months, users have created more than 7 million apps, with the number doubling in just the last 45 days.

NM-how-to-build-the-future-max-hodak.title: How To Build The Future: Max Hodak
NM-how-to-build-the-future-max-hodak.author: Garry Tan
NM-how-to-build-the-future-max-hodak.description: YC alum Max Hodak is the co-founder of Neuralink and founder of Science, a company building brain-computer interfaces that can restore sight. In this episode of How to Build the Future, we look at how BCIs work, what it takes to engineer the brain, and why brain-computer interfaces may become one of the most important technologies of the next decade.

NL-common-mistakes-with-vibe-coded-websi.title: Common Mistakes With Vibe Coded Websites
NL-common-mistakes-with-vibe-coded-websi.author: Aaron Epstein

NK-the-ai-agent-economy-is-here.title: The AI Agent Economy Is Here
NK-the-ai-agent-economy-is-here.author: Y Combinator
NK-the-ai-agent-economy-is-here.description: With the takeoff of OpenClaw and MoltBook, a new agent-driven economy is taking shape. We took a look at the explosive growth of AI dev tools and whether the time has come for builders to make something agents want.

NJ-inside-claude-code-with-its-creator-b.title: Inside Claude Code With Its Creator Boris Cherny
NJ-inside-claude-code-with-its-creator-b.author: Y Combinator
NJ-inside-claude-code-with-its-creator-b.description: A very special guest on this episode of the Lightcone! Boris Cherny, the creator of Claude Code, sits down to share the incredible journey of developing one of the most transformative coding tools of the AI era.

NH-the-new-way-to-build-a-startup.title: The New Way To Build A Startup
NH-the-new-way-to-build-a-startup.author: Garry Tan
NH-the-new-way-to-build-a-startup.description: In the AI era, startups aren't winning by hiring faster — they're winning by automating as many internal functions as possible. Garry breaks down how tiny teams are beating companies 20x their size by building automations into every workflow, from engineering to ops to customer support.

NG-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-coding-age.title: How To Get The Most Out Of Coding Agents
NG-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-coding-age.author: Y Combinator
NG-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-coding-age.description: Calvin French-Owen — a co-founder of Segment and former engineer on OpenAI's Codex team — joins us to talk about why coding agents suddenly feel so powerful, the differences between Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor, and what the future of work will look like.

NF-why-your-startup-website-isn-t-conver.title: Why Your Startup Website Isn't Converting
NF-why-your-startup-website-isn-t-conver.author: Aaron Epstein
NF-why-your-startup-website-isn-t-conver.description: YC’s Aaron Epstein sat down with Framer co-founder and CEO Jorn van Dijk to review user-submitted websites built using Framer. They share practical advice on how to clearly communicate what your product does, when animation helps (and when it hurts), and how to use Framer’s powerful tools without ending up with a site that feels generic or overly templatized.

NE-the-10-lines-of-code-behind-ai-image-.title: The 10 Lines of Code Behind AI Image Gen
NE-the-10-lines-of-code-behind-ai-image-.author: Y Combinator
NE-the-10-lines-of-code-behind-ai-image-.description: Diffusion is the foundational machine learning framework behind state-of-the-art AI image and video generation, including Sora, Midjourney and Google Veo. In this episode of Decoded, YC General Partner Ankit Gupta sits down with YC Visiting Partner Francois Chaubard to discuss how diffusion works, walk through a code sample, and explain why everyone training models should understand it.

ND-how-to-get-your-first-users.title: How To Get Your First Users
ND-how-to-get-your-first-users.author: Ankit Gupta
ND-how-to-get-your-first-users.description: When you're starting out, it isn’t enough to just build a minimum viable product. You also need a minimum evolvable product - one that can adapt to the needs of those critical early customers.

NC-inside-the-startup-building-reusable-.title: Inside The Startup Building Reusable Rockets
NC-inside-the-startup-building-reusable-.author: Y Combinator
NC-inside-the-startup-building-reusable-.description: Stoke Space is racing to build the world's first fully reusable rockets that can launch, survive reentry, and fly again and again.

NB-what-surprised-us-most-in-2025.title: What Surprised Us Most In 2025
NB-what-surprised-us-most-in-2025.author: Y Combinator
NB-what-surprised-us-most-in-2025.description: 2025 was the year AI stopped feeling chaotic and started feeling buildable. In this Lightcone episode, the YC partners break down the surprises of the year, from shifting model dominance to why the real opportunity is moving back to the application layer, and why the next wave of AI startups may be just getting started.

NA-from-pivot-hell-to-1-4-billion-unicor.title: From Pivot Hell To $1.4 Billion Unicorn
NA-from-pivot-hell-to-1-4-billion-unicor.author: Y Combinator
NA-from-pivot-hell-to-1-4-billion-unicor.description: In just a few years, James Hawkins took PostHog from an idea hacked together right before YC's W20 deadline to a unicorn powering product analytics for thousands of teams.

N9-what-founders-have-to-unlearn-to-beco.title: What Founders Have To Unlearn To Become Great CEOs
N9-what-founders-have-to-unlearn-to-beco.author: Y Combinator
N9-what-founders-have-to-unlearn-to-beco.description: Spenser Skates has spent more than a decade building Amplitude from a YC startup into a public company, and in that time, he's had to reinvent himself just as much as the product.

N8-cursor-head-of-design-reviews-startup.title: Cursor Head of Design Reviews Startup Websites
N8-cursor-head-of-design-reviews-startup.author: Aaron Epstein
N8-cursor-head-of-design-reviews-startup.description: Cursor Head of Design Ryo Lu has spent his career at the intersection of design and engineering—from building fan sites as a kid to designing products at Stripe, Asana, and Notion. Now he's rethinking how software itself gets made.  On this episode of Design Review, Ryo joins YC's Aaron Epstein to break down how great product websites communicate what a company does.

N7-ai-is-eating-logistics.title: AI Is Eating Logistics
N7-ai-is-eating-logistics.author: Y Combinator
N7-ai-is-eating-logistics.description: Ryan Petersen, founder & CEO of Flexport, joins the Lightcone to break down how AI is finally touching the physical world: making shipping cheaper, speeding up global trade, and automating work that used to live inside emails, spreadsheets, and phone calls.

N6-inside-the-startup-launching-ai-into-.title: Inside The Startup Launching AI Into Space
N6-inside-the-startup-launching-ai-into-.author: Aaron Epstein
N6-inside-the-startup-launching-ai-into-.description: Starcloud recently made history by launching a satellite with an NVIDIA H100 into orbit — the first time a GPU that powerful has ever operated in space. It's the first step toward building AI data centers in orbit, powered by continuous sunlight and cooled by radiating heat into deep space.

N5-how-to-recruit-the-best-engineers-and.title: How To Recruit The Best Engineers And Sales Reps
N5-how-to-recruit-the-best-engineers-and.author: Y Combinator
N5-how-to-recruit-the-best-engineers-and.description: Juicebox co-founder & CEO David Paffenholz joins YC's Harj Taggar to share how early-stage founders can find, pitch, and close top engineering and sales talent— from crafting better outreach to winning great hires from Big Tech— even when you're an unknown startup.

N4-inside-the-mit-ai-study-everyone-misu.title: Inside The MIT AI Study Everyone Misunderstood (And What It Means For Startups)
N4-inside-the-mit-ai-study-everyone-misu.author: Y Combinator
N4-inside-the-mit-ai-study-everyone-misu.description: Garry, Harj, Diana, and Jared break down what the study really says, why in-house enterprise AI efforts keep stalling, and how startups are filling the gap with products that learn, integrate, and actually deliver value.

N3-transformers-explained-the-discovery-.title: Transformers Explained: The Discovery That Changed AI Forever
N3-transformers-explained-the-discovery-.author: Ankit Gupta
N3-transformers-explained-the-discovery-.description: YC's Ankit Gupta traces how AI learned to understand language — from early RNNs and LSTMs to attention mechanisms and the breakthrough 2017 paper Attention Is All You Need — the discovery that unlocked the modern AI era.

N2-yc-partners-answer-your-questions-off.title: YC Partners Answer Your Questions | Office Hours
N2-yc-partners-answer-your-questions-off.author: Y Combinator
N2-yc-partners-answer-your-questions-off.description: In this episode of Office Hours, YC partners Pete, Brad, Nico, and Gustaf answer real questions from founders and share stories about how great teams build conviction, learn faster, and make better decisions as they grow.

N1-what-everyone-is-getting-wrong-about-.title: What Everyone Is Getting Wrong About AI And Jobs
N1-what-everyone-is-getting-wrong-about-.author: Garry Tan
N1-what-everyone-is-getting-wrong-about-.description: For years, we've heard two major narratives about AI. One predicting the end of human work, the other dismissing it as hype. The truth is more nuanced, and more hopeful. Garry explores what history, economics, and real companies show us— that technology doesn't replace people, it redefines what we can do.

N0-unpopular-ideas-that-became-billion-d.title: Unpopular Ideas That Became Billion-Dollar Businesses
N0-unpopular-ideas-that-became-billion-d.author: Y Combinator
N0-unpopular-ideas-that-became-billion-d.description: Garry, Harj, Jared, and Diana talk about contrarian bets — the ideas that look impossible until they work. From Uber and Coinbase to DoorDash and Flock Safety, they share how founders find opportunity where others see dead ends.

Mz-the-world-s-first-commercial-mobile-c.title: The World's First Commercial Mobile Carbon Capture Device
Mz-the-world-s-first-commercial-mobile-c.author: Y Combinator
Mz-the-world-s-first-commercial-mobile-c.description: Paul Gross and his team at Remora are trying to do something that’s never been done before. They're building mobile carbon capture devices for commercial trucks and trains—capturing CO2 from moving vehicles before it enters the atmosphere, then turning those emissions into revenue by selling it to customers that can turn the liquified CO2 into new products.

My-every-ai-founder-should-be-asking-the.title: Every AI Founder Should Be Asking These Questions
My-every-ai-founder-should-be-asking-the.author: Y Combinator
My-every-ai-founder-should-be-asking-the.description: Jordan Fisher is the co-founder & CEO of Standard AI and now leads an AI alignment research team at Anthropic. In his talk at AI Startup School, he frames the future of startups through questions rather than answers—asking how founders should navigate a world where AGI may be just a few years away.

Mx-the-7-most-powerful-moats-for-ai-star.title: The 7 Most Powerful Moats For AI Startup
Mx-the-7-most-powerful-moats-for-ai-star.author: Y Combinator
Mx-the-7-most-powerful-moats-for-ai-star.description: In this episode of Lightcone, we dive into Hamilton Helmer’s Seven Powers framework to find out how these timeless business strategies hold up in today's world of AI startups.

Mw-how-to-train-an-llm-with-anthropic-s-.title: How To Train An LLM with Anthropic's Head of Pretraining
Mw-how-to-train-an-llm-with-anthropic-s-.author: Y Combinator
Mw-how-to-train-an-llm-with-anthropic-s-.description: YC's Ankit Gupta sits down with Nick Joseph, Anthropic's Head of Pre-training, to explore the engineering challenges behind training Claude—from managing thousands of GPUs and debugging cursed bugs to balancing compute between pre-training and RL.

Mv-fintech-3-0-now-is-the-best-time-to-b.title: Fintech 3.0: Now Is The Best Time To Build In Crypto
Mv-fintech-3-0-now-is-the-best-time-to-b.author: Harj Taggar
Mv-fintech-3-0-now-is-the-best-time-to-b.description: In this episode of Main Function, YC's Harj Taggar and Base's Jesse Pollak sat down to discuss what kinds of companies they're most excited to see, why this is such an exciting time in crypto, and what the future could look like onchain.

Mu-the-future-of-software-creation-with-.title: The Future of Software Creation with Replit CEO Amjad Masad
Mu-the-future-of-software-creation-with-.author: Y Combinator
Mu-the-future-of-software-creation-with-.description: Amjad traces the arc of computing from mainframes to personal computers to a future where AI agents can create software on demand. He explains why the value of traditional software will approach zero, fundamentally reshaping how companies are built and how work gets done.

Mt-the-fde-playbook-for-ai-startups-with.title: The FDE Playbook for AI Startups with Bob McGrew
Mt-the-fde-playbook-for-ai-startups-with.author: Y Combinator
Mt-the-fde-playbook-for-ai-startups-with.description: Bob McGrew, who helped shape PayPal, Palantir, and OpenAI—where he led the development of ChatGPT, GPT-4, and the o1 reasoning model—joins The Lightcone to share how his Forward Deployed Engineer model shaped today’s AI boom, why “doing things that don’t scale at scale” works, and where the biggest opportunities lie for founders in AI.

Ms-michael-truell-building-cursor-at-23-.title: Michael Truell: Building Cursor at 23, Taking on GitHub Copilot, and Advice to Engineering Students
Ms-michael-truell-building-cursor-at-23-.author: Y Combinator
Ms-michael-truell-building-cursor-at-23-.description: In this fireside chat with YC General Partner Diana Hu, he shares the lessons that came from years of failed projects with his co-founders, why he believes programming is still essential even as AI changes how we code, and how Cursor is taking on GitHub Copilot with the conviction that all of software development will flow through models.

Mr-gpt-oss-vs-qwen-vs-deepseek-comparing.title: GPT-OSS vs. Qwen vs. Deepseek: Comparing Open Source LLM Architectures
Mr-gpt-oss-vs-qwen-vs-deepseek-comparing.author: Ankit Gupta
Mr-gpt-oss-vs-qwen-vs-deepseek-comparing.description: OpenAI recently released its first open-weights model since GPT-2, entering a field led by DeepSeek and Alibaba's Qwen. YC's Ankit Gupta breaks down everything you need to know about these top OSS models, including what sets them apart under the hood.

Mq-how-this-25-year-old-built-a-675m-leg.title: How This 25-Year-Old Built A $675M Legal AI Startup (With No Legal Experience)
Mq-how-this-25-year-old-built-a-675m-leg.author: Y Combinator
Mq-how-this-25-year-old-built-a-675m-leg.description: At 23, with no legal background, Max Junestrand co-founded Legora to transform how lawyers work. Today, Legora’s AI workspace is used by tens of thousands of lawyers, including top law firms like Goodwin and Cleary Gottlieb— and is valued at $675M just 13 months after launch.

Mp-anthropic-co-founder-building-claude-.title: Anthropic Co-founder: Building Claude Code, Lessons From GPT-3 & LLM System Design
Mp-anthropic-co-founder-building-claude-.author: Y Combinator
Mp-anthropic-co-founder-building-claude-.description: Tom Brown discusses his unconventional path from YC founder to AI researcher, the discovery of scaling laws that changed everything, and his advice for young engineers entering AI today.

Mo-the-sales-playbook-for-founders.title: The Sales Playbook for Founders
Mo-the-sales-playbook-for-founders.author: Tom Blomfield
Mo-the-sales-playbook-for-founders.description: Drawing from his experience founding Monzo and GoCardless, YC's Tom Blomfield shares his playbook for running a tight sales process that lands real, recurring revenue.

Mn-figma-ceo-dylan-field-how-ai-will-tra.title: Figma CEO Dylan Field: How AI Will Transform Design
Mn-figma-ceo-dylan-field-how-ai-will-tra.author: Y Combinator
Mn-figma-ceo-dylan-field-how-ai-will-tra.description: Dylan Field co-founded Figma to bring the design process online and make it multiplayer. From a meme maker built on WebGL to a design platform powering millions, Figma’s journey hit a major milestone with its IPO last week.

Mm-the-finance-startup-bringing-agentic-.title: The Finance Startup Bringing Agentic AI to Wall Street
Mm-the-finance-startup-bringing-agentic-.author: Y Combinator
Mm-the-finance-startup-bringing-agentic-.description: Brothers Chaz and Arnie Englander started Model ML after building and selling two YC companies. Now, their third company, Model ML, is a full AI-powered workspace purpose-built for financial services, empowering firms to create automations and workflows that reflect exactly how their teams operate. And it's already being used by 10% of the world's top investment banks and private equity firms.

Ml-scaling-and-the-road-to-human-level-a.title: Scaling and the Road to Human-Level AI | Anthropic Co-founder Jared Kaplan
Ml-scaling-and-the-road-to-human-level-a.author: Y Combinator
Ml-scaling-and-the-road-to-human-level-a.description: Jared Kaplan helped uncover one of AI’s most surprising truths: that intelligence scales in a predictable, almost physical way. That insight became foundational to the modern era of large language models—and led him to co-found Anthropic.

Mk-brand-design-tips-from-linear-founder.title: Brand Design Tips From Linear Founder Karri Saarinen
Mk-brand-design-tips-from-linear-founder.author: Aaron Epstein
Mk-brand-design-tips-from-linear-founder.description: For Design Review, Aaron is joined by Karri Saarinen, co-founder & CEO of Linear, to break down how today’s best software companies design their sites.

Mj-chelsea-finn-building-robots-that-can.title: Chelsea Finn: Building Robots That Can Do Anything
Mj-chelsea-finn-building-robots-that-can.author: Y Combinator
Mj-chelsea-finn-building-robots-that-can.description: Chelsea Finn has built her career around teaching machines how to learn. A pioneer of meta‑learning methods, she’s now using that foundation to bring learning-driven robotics into messy, real-world environments rather than confined lab setups.

Mi-replit-ceo-amjad-masad-coding-agents-.title: Replit CEO Amjad Masad: Coding Agents, Autonomy, and the Future of Work
Mi-replit-ceo-amjad-masad-coding-agents-.author: Y Combinator
Mi-replit-ceo-amjad-masad-coding-agents-.description: Amjad Masad started Replit to make programming accessible to anyone, anywhere. What began as a tool for learning to code has grown into a platform pushing the limits of AI-assisted software creation and recently surpassed $100M in ARR.

Mh-john-jumper-alphafold-and-the-future-.title: John Jumper: AlphaFold and the Future of Science
Mh-john-jumper-alphafold-and-the-future-.author: Y Combinator
Mh-john-jumper-alphafold-and-the-future-.description: John Jumper is a physicist-turned-computational biologist who led DeepMind’s AlphaFold team—and earned the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for solving protein folding, a decades-old scientific challenge.

Mg-aravind-srinivas-the-race-to-build-th.title: Aravind Srinivas: The Race to Build the AI Browser of the Future
Mg-aravind-srinivas-the-race-to-build-th.author: Y Combinator
Mg-aravind-srinivas-the-race-to-build-th.description: Aravind Srinivas started Perplexity with one goal: to rethink how we search, browse, and interact with information online. In this fireside, he shares the journey from hacking together a natural-language-to-SQL search tool to building a product used by millions around the world.

Mf-andrew-ng-building-faster-with-ai.title: Andrew Ng: Building Faster with AI
Mf-andrew-ng-building-faster-with-ai.author: Y Combinator
Mf-andrew-ng-building-faster-with-ai.description: Andrew Ng has helped shape some of the most influential movements in modern AI—from online education to deep learning to AI entrepreneurship. In this talk, he shares what he’s learning now: why execution speed matters more than ever, how agentic workflows are changing what startups can build, and why concreteness beats vagueness when turning ideas into products.

Me-how-to-spend-your-20s-in-the-ai-era.title: How to Spend Your 20s in the AI Era
Me-how-to-spend-your-20s-in-the-ai-era.author: Y Combinator
Me-how-to-spend-your-20s-in-the-ai-era.description: In a special Lightcone Live at AI Startup School, Garry, Diana, Harj, and Jared discuss why it's now more important than ever to focus on building real skills, domain expertise, and agency rather than just chasing credentials.

Md-fran-ois-chollet-the-arc-prize-how-we.title: François Chollet: The ARC Prize & How We Get to AGI
Md-fran-ois-chollet-the-arc-prize-how-we.author: Y Combinator
Md-fran-ois-chollet-the-arc-prize-how-we.description: A leading voice in AI, François Chollet has spent years thinking deeply about what intelligence actually is, and why scaling up today’s AI models isn’t enough to reach it. In this talk, he walks through the limits of pretraining and memorized skills, and lays out a path toward true general intelligence—AI that can adapt on the fly, reason in new situations, and invent novel solutions.

Mb-fei-fei-li-spatial-intelligence-is-th.title: Fei-Fei Li: Spatial Intelligence is the Next Frontier in AI
Mb-fei-fei-li-spatial-intelligence-is-th.author: Y Combinator
Mb-fei-fei-li-spatial-intelligence-is-th.description: Dr. Fei-Fei Li is often called the godmother of AI—and for good reason. Before the world had AI as we know it, she was helping build the foundation. In this fireside at AI Startup School, she dives into the origin of computer vision and why spatial intelligence is the next frontier.

MZ-satya-nadella-microsoft-s-ai-bets-hyp.title: Satya Nadella: Microsoft's AI Bets, Hyperscaling, Quantum Computing Breakthroughs
MZ-satya-nadella-microsoft-s-ai-bets-hyp.author: Y Combinator
MZ-satya-nadella-microsoft-s-ai-bets-hyp.description: Microsoft Chairman & CEO Satya Nadella shares how Microsoft is thinking about this moment— from the infrastructure needed to train frontier models, to the social permission required to use that compute.

MY-sam-altman-the-future-of-openai-chatg.title: Sam Altman: The Future of OpenAI, ChatGPT's Origins, and Building AI Hardware
MY-sam-altman-the-future-of-openai-chatg.author: Y Combinator

MX-elon-musk-digital-superintelligence-m.title: Elon Musk: Digital Superintelligence, Multiplanetary Life, How to Be Useful
MX-elon-musk-digital-superintelligence-m.author: Y Combinator

MW-andrej-karpathy-software-is-changing-.title: Andrej Karpathy: Software Is Changing (Again)
MW-andrej-karpathy-software-is-changing-.author: Y Combinator
MW-andrej-karpathy-software-is-changing-.description: Drawing on his work at Stanford, OpenAI, and Tesla, Andrej sees a shift underway. We’ve entered the era of “Software 3.0,” where natural language becomes the new programming interface and models do the rest.

MV-alexandr-wang-building-scale-ai-trans.title: Alexandr Wang: Building Scale AI, Transforming Work with Agents & Competing With China
MV-alexandr-wang-building-scale-ai-trans.author: Y Combinator
MV-alexandr-wang-building-scale-ai-trans.description: Today, Scale AI powers a multi-hundred-million dollar engine behind America's AI infrastructure— fueling everything from Fortune 500 workflows to real-time military planning. Founder Alexandr Wang joined us on the Lightcone to share how Scale evolved from a scrappy YC startup into the backbone of some of the world's most advanced AI systems.

MU-cursor-ceo-going-beyond-code-superint.title: Cursor CEO: Going Beyond Code, Superintelligent AI Agents And Why Taste Still Matters
MU-cursor-ceo-going-beyond-code-superint.author: Garry Tan
MU-cursor-ceo-going-beyond-code-superint.description: Michael Truell, co-founder and CEO of Anysphere, the company behind @Cursor_ai, joined @garrytan to talk about building one of the fastest-growing startups of all time—and why he's betting everything on a world beyond code.

MT-fusion-energy-is-tech-s-next-big-unlo.title: Fusion Energy Is Tech's Next Big Unlock
MT-fusion-energy-is-tech-s-next-big-unlo.author: Gustaf Alströmer
MT-fusion-energy-is-tech-s-next-big-unlo.description: Gustaf explains the history of fusion, the physics behind it, and the engineering challenges that stalled it for nearly a century. He also looks at how Helion Energy is approaching the problem differently, as they develop a new fusion system expected to deliver power to Microsoft by 2028.

MS-state-of-the-art-prompting-for-ai-age.title: State-Of-The-Art Prompting For AI Agents
MS-state-of-the-art-prompting-for-ai-age.author: Y Combinator
MS-state-of-the-art-prompting-for-ai-age.description: Garry, Harj, Diana, and Jared break down what they've learned from working with hundreds of founders building with LLMs: why prompting still matters, where it breaks down, and how teams are making it more reliable in production.

MR-ai-apps-are-broken-here-s-how-to-fix-.title: AI Apps Are Broken — Here's How To Fix Them
MR-ai-apps-are-broken-here-s-how-to-fix-.author: Tom Blomfield, David Lieb, Pete Koomen
MR-ai-apps-are-broken-here-s-how-to-fix-.description: Tom, Dave, and Pete break down what it means to build AI-native software—and why the future belongs to products that let users teach machines how they think.

MQ-startup-ideas-you-can-now-build-with-.title: Startup Ideas You Can Now Build With AI
MQ-startup-ideas-you-can-now-build-with-.author: Y Combinator
MQ-startup-ideas-you-can-now-build-with-.description: Garry, Harj, Diana and Jared talk through the kinds of startups that are suddenly viable thanks to LLMs—from full-stack law firms to personalized tutors to recruiting platforms that can finally scale.

MP-how-ai-coding-agents-will-change-your.title: How AI Coding Agents Will Change Your Job
MP-how-ai-coding-agents-will-change-your.author: Tom Blomfield, David Lieb
MP-how-ai-coding-agents-will-change-your.description: Tom and David discuss how AI coding tools are transforming software development, why small, high-agency teams will be able to do what once took armies of engineers, and why there's never been a better time to start something new.

MO-windsurf-ceo-betting-on-ai-agents-piv.title: Windsurf CEO: Betting On AI Agents, Pivoting In 48 Hours, And The Future of Coding
MO-windsurf-ceo-betting-on-ai-agents-piv.author: Y Combinator
MO-windsurf-ceo-betting-on-ai-agents-piv.description: Varun Mohan shares the inside story of how they pivoted at the edge of failure, trained new models from scratch, outpaced giants like GitHub Copilot, and what the future of building with AI looks like.

MN-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-vibe-codin.title: How To Get The Most Out Of Vibe Coding
MN-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-vibe-codin.author: Tom Blomfield
MN-how-to-get-the-most-out-of-vibe-codin.description: From writing full-stack apps to debugging with a single paste of an error message, AI is becoming a legit collaborator in the dev process. This video is a playbook for anyone who wants to get the most out of vibe coding and build faster.

MM-the-next-breakthrough-in-ai-agents-is.title: The Next Breakthrough In AI Agents Is Here
MM-the-next-breakthrough-in-ai-agents-is.author: Garry Tan
MM-the-next-breakthrough-in-ai-agents-is.description: Usable AI agents are finally here. Garry dives into Manus’ “chain-of-thought injection” technique that enables agents to actively update plans, what works particularly well with its UI, and how founders can create enduring value with application-level products.

ML-how-zepto-became-india-s-fastest-grow.title: How Zepto Became India’s Fastest Growing Startup
ML-how-zepto-became-india-s-fastest-grow.author: Garry Tan
ML-how-zepto-became-india-s-fastest-grow.description: Imagine ordering groceries and having them show up at your doorstep in just 10 minutes. That’s the promise of Zepto, the fastest-growing e-commerce company in India. Garry sits down with co-founder and CEO Aadit to unpack how a WhatsApp group during the pandemic turned into one of India’s fastest-growing internet companies.

MK-why-every-founder-should-care-about-d.title: Why Every Founder Should Care About Design
MK-why-every-founder-should-care-about-d.author: Aaron Epstein
MK-why-every-founder-should-care-about-d.description: More founders should care about design in the earliest days of their startup. In this special episode of Design Review, Aaron sits down with Raphael Schaad, the Head of Calendar at Notion to discuss his leap from designer to founder, why illustration is an important first step when building a new product, and what ultimately makes designers uniquely qualified to start a company today.

MJ-how-to-build-the-future-tony-xu.title: How To Build The Future: Tony Xu
MJ-how-to-build-the-future-tony-xu.author: Garry Tan
MJ-how-to-build-the-future-tony-xu.description: Twelve years after starting DoorDash, co-founder & CEO Tony Xu and the entire team still personally make deliveries each year, staying true to their core value: customer obsession. Today, DoorDash is the largest food delivery platform in the US. Tony explains how their long-term vision won — helping create a new market while surviving near death and a global pandemic along the way.

Km-how-to-survive-the-crypto-boom-bust-c.title: How to Survive the Crypto Boom & Bust Cycle
Km-how-to-survive-the-crypto-boom-bust-c.author: Garry Tan

Kk-why-founders-shouldn-t-think-like-inv.title: Why Founders Shouldn't Think Like Investors

Kj-where-is-the-real-cerebral-valley-lig.title: Where Is The REAL Cerebral Valley? [Lightcone Podcast Ep. 3]
Kj-where-is-the-real-cerebral-valley-lig.author: Y Combinator
Kj-where-is-the-real-cerebral-valley-lig.description: On this latest episode of the Lightcone Podcast, Garry, Diana, Harj and Jared talk about “Cerebral Valley” and why San Francisco has become THE place to build an AI company.

Kh-day-in-the-life-of-y-combinator-presi.title: Day in the Life of Y Combinator President & CEO Garry Tan during the first week of the batch
Kh-day-in-the-life-of-y-combinator-presi.author: Garry Tan
Kh-day-in-the-life-of-y-combinator-presi.description: Come behind the scenes of Y Combinator during the very first week of the W24 batch.

Kg-building-confidence-in-yourself-and-y.title: Building Confidence In Yourself and Your Ideas
Kg-building-confidence-in-yourself-and-y.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel

Ke-the-secrets-of-y-combinator-s-early-s.title: The Secrets of Y Combinator’s Early Success (with YC Co-Founder Jessica Livingston)

Kd-apple-vision-pro-startup-platform-of-.title: Apple Vision Pro: Startup Platform Of The Future? [Lightcone Podcast Ep. 2]
Kd-apple-vision-pro-startup-platform-of-.author: Y Combinator
Kd-apple-vision-pro-startup-platform-of-.description: In this episode of the Lightcone Podcast, YC Group Partners discuss the launch of the Apple Vision Pro and the potential of this new platform for startups.

Kc-stop-innovating-on-the-wrong-things.title: Stop Innovating (On The Wrong Things)
Kc-stop-innovating-on-the-wrong-things.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel

Kb-the-truth-about-building-ai-startups-.title: The Truth About Building AI Startups Today [Lightcone Podcast Ep. 1]
Kb-the-truth-about-building-ai-startups-.author: Y Combinator

KZ-should-your-startup-bootstrap-or-rais.title: Should your startup bootstrap or raise venture capital?
KZ-should-your-startup-bootstrap-or-rais.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel
KZ-should-your-startup-bootstrap-or-rais.description: In this episode of Dalton & Michael, we’ll explain why this particular debate is a much simpler one than many make it out to be.

KY-backstory-tom-blomfield.title: Backstory: Tom Blomfield
KY-backstory-tom-blomfield.author: Y Combinator
KY-backstory-tom-blomfield.description:
There aren’t a ton of people in the world who can say they founded a billion dollar company. There are far fewer that can say they did it twice.

YC Group Partner Tom Blomfield is one of them — and this is his story.

KV-do-technical-founders-need-a-business.title: Do technical founders need a business co-founder?
KV-do-technical-founders-need-a-business.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel
KV-do-technical-founders-need-a-business.description: Every software company needs a technical co-founder. But what kind of co-founder does the technical founder need?

KT-consumer-startup-metrics.title: Consumer Startup Metrics
KT-consumer-startup-metrics.author: Tom Blomfield
KT-consumer-startup-metrics.description: In this episode of Startup School, YC Group Partner Tom Blomfield dives deeper into the metrics that matter most for consumer startups.

KS-the-asymmetric-upside-of-being-positi.title: The asymmetric upside of being positive
KS-the-asymmetric-upside-of-being-positi.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel
KS-the-asymmetric-upside-of-being-positi.description: In this episode of Dalton and Michael, we’ll discuss the best ways for founders to think about weighing optimism and pessimism in their day-to-day lives.

KR-key-startup-metrics.title: Key Startup Metrics
KR-key-startup-metrics.author: Tom Blomfield
KR-key-startup-metrics.description: YC Group Partner Tom Blomfield discusses one of the most important elements of running any startup: metrics! Tom shares what key metrics to track and how to use them to make the best decisions for your company.

KQ-garry-tan-speaks-at-stanford-s-entrep.title: Garry Tan speaks at Stanford's Entrepreneurial Thought Leaders (ETL) lecture series

KP-five-devtool-startups-get-their-websi.title: Five DevTool Startups Get Their Websites Reviewed
KP-five-devtool-startups-get-their-websi.author: Aaron Epstein

One of the best ways to do that is with a well designed website. For this episode of Design Review, Aaron Epstein and David Siegel will break down what that means.

KO-does-your-tech-startup-really-need-a-.title: Does your tech startup really need a technical co-founder? Yes.
KO-does-your-tech-startup-really-need-a-.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel

KM-emmett-shear-co-founder-of-twitch.title: Emmett Shear, Co-Founder of Twitch
KM-emmett-shear-co-founder-of-twitch.author: The Social Radars

KL-33-ai-startup-founders-debate-the-cre.title: 33 AI Startup Founders Debate the Creation of Artificial General Intelligence
KL-33-ai-startup-founders-debate-the-cre.author: Y Combinator

KK-how-psychedelics-will-change-society-.title: How psychedelics will change society for the better

KJ-the-10-year-overnight-success-story-o.title: The 10-Year “Overnight” Success Story of Casetext
KJ-the-10-year-overnight-success-story-o.author: Garry Tan

YC President Garry Tan sits down with Casetext co-founder Jake Heller to learn the real story of their 10-year “overnight” success.

KH-bill-clerico-co-founder-ceo-of-wepay.title: Bill Clerico, Co-Founder & CEO of WePay

KG-from-underdog-to-undefeated-the-edriz.title: From Underdog to Undefeated: The Edrizio De La Cruz founder story

KF-how-to-not-get-screwed-over-as-a-soft.author: Dalton Caldwell, Michael Seibel

KD-how-to-convert-more-visitors-into-cus.author: Aaron Epstein

KC-dimitri-dadiomov-co-founder-ceo-of-mo.title: Dimitri Dadiomov, Co-Founder & CEO of Modern Treasury
KC-dimitri-dadiomov-co-founder-ceo-of-mo.author: The Social Radars

K7-why-yc-we-asked-50-founders-why-they-.title: Why YC? We asked 50 founders why they applied to Y Combinator
K7-why-yc-we-asked-50-founders-why-they-.author: Y Combinator
K7-why-yc-we-asked-50-founders-why-they-.description: Why do founders apply to YC? For this episode of Founder FAQ, we asked them!

K3-brian-armstrong-co-founder-and-ceo-of.title: Brian Armstrong, Co-Founder and CEO of Coinbase
K3-brian-armstrong-co-founder-and-ceo-of.author: The Social Radars
K3-brian-armstrong-co-founder-and-ceo-of.description: In this episode of The Social Radars, Jessica Livingston and Carolynn Levy talk to Coinbase co-founder and CEO Brian Armstrong about the company’s earliest days

What society imposes on us is the wrong kind of thinking: one that is based on analogy and credentialism instead of first principles thinking.

Ja-tony-xu-co-founder-ceo-of-doordash.author: The Social Radars
Ja-tony-xu-co-founder-ceo-of-doordash.description: In this episode of The Social Radars, Jessica Livingston and Carolynn Levy talk to Tony Xu, co-founder & CEO of Doordash.

JZ-garry-tan-president-and-ceo-of-y-comb.author: The Social Radars
JZ-garry-tan-president-and-ceo-of-y-comb.description: In this episode of The Social Radars, Jessica Livingston and Carolynn Levy talk to Garry Tan, President and CEO of Y Combinator.

JY-tracy-young-co-founder-ceo-of-plangri.description: In this episode of The Social Radars, Jessica Livingston and Carolynn Levy talk to Tracy Young, Co-Founder of PlanGrid

JW-brian-chesky-co-founder-ceo-of-airbnb.description: Jessica Livingston and Carolynn Levy, talk to Brian Chesky, Co-Founder and CEO of Airbnb.
